chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Cr. Bail Appln. No.1279 of 2025 ( Muhammad Salman v. The State ) ____________________________________________________ DATE: ORDER WITH SIGNATURE(s) OF JUDGE(s) ____________________________________________________ 1. For Orders on Office Objection 2. For hearing of Bail Application 23-06-2025 Mr. Zia-ul-Haq Makhdoom, Advocate for Applicant a/w Applicant (on bail). Mr. Muhammad Haseeb Jamali, Advocate for Complainant. Mr. Muhammad Mohsin Mangi, APG ---------------------------- Through instant Criminal Bail Application, the Applicant/accused namely, Muhammad Salman son of Muhammad Ibrahim Gadit seeks pre-arrest bail in Crime No.532/2025 registered at police station Ferozabad under Sections 147, 148, 149, 506-B, 337A(i), 504, PPC. 2. According to the allegations set-forth in the FIR, on 15.05.2025, Complainant Ahmed Shah Mir while returning to his home, received a telephone call from his house that present Applicant/accused along with his accomplices engaged in an altercation with his father and uncle. After informing Madadgar-15, he reached at home, shortly thereafter, the Applicant/accused arrived duly armed along with 7-8 unidentified individuals, started fight and physically assaulted the Complainant with punches and kicks. Consequently sustained internal inujuries. Applicant/accused also threatened to kill him. In the meantime, his fellow advocates and nephew Syed Zainul Abideen arrived and intervened to rescue him. 3. Learned Counsel for the Applicant/accused submits that parties being neighbourers have arrived with the settlement in the interest of goodwill and in the spirit of reconciliation.

In the meantime, his fellow advocates and nephew Syed Zainul Abideen arrived and intervened to rescue him. 3. Learned Counsel for the Applicant/accused submits that parties being neighbourers have arrived with the settlement in the interest of goodwill and in the spirit of reconciliation. He further submits that the Complainant also gives No- objection for confirmation of the instant Bail Application. 4. On the other hand, learned Counsel for the Complainant frankly conceded the above situation and submitted the Statement of the Complainant for No Objection of confirmation of interim pre-arrest bail granted to the Applicant. 5. Accordingly, learned APG also recorded no-objection. 6. In view of the above, the interim pre-arrest bail granted earlier to the Applicant/accused dated 19.05.2025 is hereby confirmed on same terms and condition. The Criminal Bail Application stands disposed of accordingly. JUDGE
